pattern: Merging is confluent, so a single left-to-right pass with a stack (fusing the top two whenever they share a factor, and re-checking after each fuse) reaches the same fixed point as repeatedly scanning the whole array.
hints: The order you merge adjacent non-coprime pairs in doesn't change the final array, so you never need to rescan from the start after a merge.
Keep a stack of the results so far; when the next number shares a common factor greater than one with the stack's top, pop it, merge via LCM, and keep checking the new top against the merged value.
gcd(a, b) > 1 is the merge trigger and lcm(a, b) = a * b // gcd(a, b) is the replacement; push and repeat the check until the stack top is coprime with the current value, then push it.

edge_inputs.description: chain of merges that keeps cascading backward
repeated small values merge pairwise down to a coprime run
already fully coprime, array is unchanged
a merge produces a value that is still non-coprime with what is now two positions back
all identical values collapse to a single number via repeated self-LCM
large values whose LCM approaches the stated output bound

stress.description: long run of small factors of 2 and 3 forcing many cascading merges, capped so the merged product stays under 1e8
stress.note: small factor pool guarantees heavy adjacent-merging while keeping every intermediate LCM well under the 1e8 output guarantee
